commands.review_summary:
The branch adds range checks around DuckDuckGo numeric HTML entity decoding and a regression test for oversized decimal and hex entities while preserving valid supplementary-plane decoding.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +10. Total +16 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, not from a full live runtime run: current main funnels DuckDuckGo result title, URL, and snippet through decodeHtmlEntities, and that helper passes out-of-range numeric entities into String.fromCodePoint. A focused Node primitive check confirms 0x110000 throws RangeError.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof and routine maintainer validation, not a narrow code repair ClawSweeper can generate.

target.title: fix(duckduckgo): guard out-of-range numeric HTML entities
target.body:
## Problem
`decodeHtmlEntities` decodes numeric entities with `String.fromCodePoint(Number.parseInt(...))` without a range check, so an out-of-range entity such as `&#99999999;` or `�` throws `RangeError` and makes the whole results page fail to parse (title/url/snippet all go through it).

## Fix
Validate the code point is within `0..0x10FFFF` and keep the original entity text otherwise.

## Proof
Added a regression in `extensions/duckduckgo/src/ddg-search-provider.test.ts` covering decimal and hex out-of-range entities (no throw, text preserved) plus a valid supplementary-plane entity that still decodes — fails before, passes after. Pure unit test.
target.author: llagy009
